name: ClawMax System Test
description: Full platform validation template. Tests agent chat, group chat, workflows, DAG execution, filesystem access, and GitHub coordination. When all tests pass, the platform is confirmed working.
author: ClawMax Team

agents.name: Test Lead
agents.role: Test orchestrator — coordinates test execution, compiles results, reports pass/fail status. You are responsible for verifying that all platform features work correctly.

agents.name: Test Agent
agents.role: Test executor — runs assigned test tasks, writes output files, reports results to groups. Follow instructions precisely and report pass/fail for each test step.

communities.name: Test Team
communities.description: System test community — all test agents coordinate here

groups.name: Test Chat
groups.description: Group chat testing — all agents participate in conversations

workflows.name: Test Kickoff
workflows.description: Tier 1: Verify agents are online and can respond
workflows.content:
# System Test Kickoff

You are part of the ClawMax system test team.

## Platform Health Check
1. Confirm your agent ID and role
2. List the groups and communities you belong to
3. List your installed skills
4. Say 'READY' to confirm you are operational

## Test Configuration
> **Customize before running:**

- **GitHub repo:** [e.g., Maximilien-ai/clawmax-test]
- **Test mode:** [basic, full]

Keep response under 100 words.

workflows.name: Test Filesystem
workflows.description: Tier 1: Test file read/write in shared workspace
workflows.content:
# Filesystem Test

Test that agents can read and write files in the workspace.

## Test Steps
1. Write a file called `test-output-{your-agent-id}.md` in your workspace directory
2. Include your agent ID, current timestamp, and the text 'FILESYSTEM TEST'
3. Read the workspace directory listing using workspace-ls skill
4. Report what files you can see
5. Say 'FILESYSTEM PASS' if you could write and read, or 'FILESYSTEM FAIL' with error details

Keep response under 100 words.

workflows.name: Test Communications
workflows.description: Tier 1: Test group chat and inter-agent messaging
workflows.content:
# Communications Test

Test that agents can contribute valid replies to the workflow's assigned group thread.

## Test Steps
1. Write the exact message ClawMax should post back into this workflow run's current group channel
2. Include your agent ID and the text 'COMMS TEST MESSAGE'
3. Briefly confirm whether you received the workflow in the current group-thread context for this run
4. Do not claim success or failure based on seeing live replies from other agents during the same run
5. Say 'COMMS PASS' if you can produce the requested group-thread reply content, or 'COMMS FAIL' only if you cannot produce that reply content

ClawMax will post your final response into the current workflow group channel automatically. Do not attempt to use a separate tool, plugin, transport, or session label unless the workflow explicitly asks you to test one.

Keep response under 80 words.

workflows.name: Test GitHub
workflows.description: Tier 2: Test GitHub issue creation and coordination
workflows.content:
# GitHub Coordination Test

Test that agents can interact with GitHub.

## Test Configuration
- **GitHub repo:** [e.g., Maximilien-ai/clawmax-test]

## Important Constraints
- Use GitHub directly via the configured repo slug and available GitHub tooling
- Do not clone the repo, create a local checkout, or make a new working directory for this test
- Do not run `git` commands unless you have already confirmed you are inside an existing git repository and the step truly requires it
- If repository access is missing, report that specific access problem instead of attempting local repo setup

## Test Steps (test-lead)
1. Create a GitHub issue titled 'System Test - {timestamp}' in the configured repo
2. Add a comment that assigns or directs test-agent1 to acknowledge the issue
3. Report the issue number and say `GITHUB PASS` or `GITHUB FAIL`

## Test Steps (test-agent1)
1. Find the latest issue created by test-lead in the configured repo
2. Add a comment: 'Test agent acknowledging assignment'
3. Report `GITHUB PASS` or `GITHUB FAIL`

Keep response under 100 words.

workflows.name: Test DAG Parallel A
workflows.description: Tier 1: Parallel execution test — runs alongside Parallel B
workflows.content:
# Parallel Test A

This tests DAG parallel execution.
1. Say 'PARALLEL A RUNNING'
2. Write 'parallel-a-complete' to a file called `test-parallel-a.txt`
3. Say 'PARALLEL A COMPLETE'

Keep response under 30 words.

workflows.name: Test DAG Parallel B
workflows.description: Tier 1: Parallel execution test — runs alongside Parallel A
workflows.content:
# Parallel Test B

This tests DAG parallel execution.
1. Say 'PARALLEL B RUNNING'
2. Write 'parallel-b-complete' to a file called `test-parallel-b.txt`
3. Say 'PARALLEL B COMPLETE'

Keep response under 30 words.

workflows.name: Test Report
workflows.description: Final: Compile test results across all tiers
